sma.definition: Arithmetic mean of price over N periods. Key SMAs: 21, 50, 100, 150 (primary stage anchor), 200.

ema.definition: Weighted average giving more weight to recent prices. Key EMAs: 10 (short-term trend), 21, 200.
ema.formula: EMA = Price × k + EMA(prev) × (1 - k), where k = 2/(n+1)

rsi.definition: Momentum oscillator (0–100) using Wilder smoothing. Above 70 = overbought, below 30 = oversold.
rsi.formula: RS = Wilder Avg Gain / Wilder Avg Loss; RSI = 100 - 100/(1+RS)

atr.definition: Volatility measure — average range of price movement. ATR%14 normalizes across price levels.

atrp.definition: ATR as a % of current price. A $10 stock with ATR $0.50 and a $500 stock with ATR $25 both have ATRP 5%.

macd.definition: Trend-following momentum indicator from two EMAs. Signal crossovers indicate momentum shifts.
macd.formula: MACD = EMA(12) - EMA(26); Signal = EMA(9) of MACD

adx.definition: Trend strength (ADX > 25 = strong trend). +DI vs -DI shows direction.

td_sequential.definition: DeMark exhaustion counter. 9-count setup suggests potential reversal.
td_sequential.formula: Buy: 9 consecutive closes below close 4 bars ago

sma150.definition: 150-day Simple Moving Average. The primary anchor for Stage Analysis classification. Price position relative to SMA150 determines base stage.

ext_pct.definition: How far price has moved from SMA150 — the primary distance metric in Stage Analysis.

atre.definition: Price distance from key MAs measured in ATRs. ATRE_150 > 6.0 triggers the 2C override.

ema10_dist_n.definition: How far price is from EMA10, normalized by ATR. Measures chase risk — values > 3.0 are extended.

sma150_slope.definition: Rate of change of the primary anchor over 20 days. Thresholds: > +0.35% = rising, < -0.35% = falling.

vol_ratio.definition: Current volume relative to 20-day average. Values > 1.5 confirm breakouts.

mansfield_rs.definition: Stock's performance vs SPY over trailing year. Positive = outperforming the market.

breakout_elite.definition: Stocks in Stage 2A/2B with RS>0, tight EMA10 distance (≤2.0 normalized), top ATRE percentile (≥70), and high 52-week range (≥60%). The best long candidates.

breakout_standard.definition: Stocks in Stage 2A/2B/2C with RS>-5, moderate EMA10 distance (≤3.0), and 52-week range ≥40%. Good quality setups with slightly relaxed criteria.

early_base.definition: Stocks in Stage 1B/2A/2B with wider EMA10 tolerance (≤4.0). Pattern is forming but breakout not yet confirmed.

speculative.definition: Stocks in Stage 1A/1B. Very early in the base-building process. Higher risk, requires patience.

breakdown_elite.definition: Stocks in Stage 4A/4B with RS<0, low 52-week range (≤30%). The best short candidates during bearish regimes.

breakdown_standard.definition: Stocks in Stage 3B/4A/4B/4C with RS<5. In distribution or decline phase, potential short candidates.

market_regime.definition: Market-wide risk state (R1–R5) from 6 macro inputs. Gates all downstream decisions including position sizing, scan tier access, and exit triggers.

r1.definition: Full risk-on environment. All long tiers accessible, full position sizing, no shorts.

r2.definition: Bull market with elevated valuations. Tiers 1-3 accessible, slightly reduced sizing.

r3.definition: Transitional market. Tiers 1-2 only, reduced sizing, short tier 1 accessible.

r4.definition: Counter-trend rally in bear market. Only Tier 1 longs, short tiers 1-2 accessible.

r5.definition: Risk-off environment. No new longs, all short tiers accessible, aggressive exits.

1a.definition: Price near flat SMA150, slope neutral or slightly negative. Very early base forming.

1b.definition: Price near SMA150 with improving slope. Base tightening, preparing for breakout.

2a.definition: Price above rising SMA150, recent breakout. Prime buying zone.

2b.definition: Price well above rising SMA150, confirmed uptrend. Pullbacks are buying opportunities.

2c.definition: Price far extended from SMA150 (ATRE > 6). High risk of pullback, reduce position sizing.

3a.definition: Price still above SMA150 but slope flattening. Warning sign, tighten stops.

3b.definition: Price losing SMA150, slope negative. Exit longs, potential short setup.

4a.definition: Price below falling SMA150. Downtrend confirmed, short candidates.

4b.definition: Price well below falling SMA150. Strong downtrend, ideal shorts.

4c.definition: Price extremely extended below SMA150 (< -15%). Potential bounce, cover shorts.
